description:
You are acting as a release-manager who is about to roll out a new application build.  
    Inside the working directory “/home/user/deploy” you will find two pre-existing configuration
    files that still point at the previous version of the software (1.2.3):

      • /home/user/deploy/config.yaml  
      • /home/user/deploy/metadata.toml  

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. In BOTH files bump the application version from 1.2.3 → 1.2.4.  
    2. Add a release date of 2023-10-10 to each file.  
       • YAML key must be release_date: "2023-10-10"  
       • TOML key must be release_date = "2023-10-10" placed inside the existing [package] table.  
    3. Create a plain-text log called /home/user/deploy/release-1.2.4.log that the CI pipeline
       will later ingest.  The file MUST contain exactly three lines, in this order, with a single
       final trailing newline and no extra spaces:

          CONFIG_YAML_VERSION=1.2.4
          METADATA_TOML_VERSION=1.2.4
          RELEASE_DATE=2023-10-10

    Success criteria that the automated test will check  
    ----------------------------------------------------  
    • /home/user/deploy/config.yaml exists and its “version” field equals "1.2.4"
      and it contains the new key release_date: "2023-10-10" directly under environment.  
    • /home/user/deploy/metadata.toml exists and its [package] table has
      version = "1.2.4" and release_date = "2023-10-10".  
    • /home/user/deploy/release-1.2.4.log exists and its content is byte-for-byte identical to
      the 3-line snippet above (including the trailing newline).  

    Keep your command sequence short (about 2–3 shell commands total).  No root privileges are
    available.  When you are done, verify the contents visually with cat or similar tools.
truth:
Initial filesystem state BEFORE the agent starts
    -----------------------------------------------
    Directory: /home/user/deploy  (already created, owned by user)

    File: /home/user/deploy/config.yaml
    -----------------------------------
    app_name: "WeatherApp"
    version: "1.2.3"
    environment: "staging"
    maintainers:
      - "alice"
      - "bob"

    File: /home/user/deploy/metadata.toml
    -------------------------------------
    [package]
    name = "weather_app"
    version = "1.2.3"

    [build]
    target = "x86_64"

    Desired filesystem state AFTER the agent finishes
    -------------------------------------------------
    File: /home/user/deploy/config.yaml
    -----------------------------------
    app_name: "WeatherApp"
    version: "1.2.4"
    environment: "staging"
    release_date: "2023-10-10"
    maintainers:
      - "alice"
      - "bob"

    File: /home/user/deploy/metadata.toml
    -------------------------------------
    [package]
    name = "weather_app"
    version = "1.2.4"
    release_date = "2023-10-10"

    [build]
    target = "x86_64"

    File: /home/user/deploy/release-1.2.4.log
    ----------------------------------------
    CONFIG_YAML_VERSION=1.2.4
    METADATA_TOML_VERSION=1.2.4
    RELEASE_DATE=2023-10-10

    (Note: file ends with a single trailing newline.)

    The automated grader will diff these exact file contents.
